tests.name: init_sh_non_interactive_exits_zero
tests.details: init.sh --non-interactive returned 0 with --git-host other + fake remote

tests.name: release_workflow_created
tests.details: release.yml not laid down. init.sh:2308 short-circuits when git_host=other (no template selected). Documented runbook expectation does not match implementation for --git-host other.

tests.name: context7_detection
tests.details: verify-install.sh reports Context7 MCP installed and configured

tests.name: platform_module_present
tests.details: expected docs/platform-modules/mcp_server.md but framework ships docs/platform-modules/mcp-server.md (hyphen vs underscore). init.sh:1124 looks for ${PLATFORM}.md but PLATFORM=mcp_server while file uses hyphen. verify-install.sh also flags this manual issue.

tests.name: framework_self_guard_active
tests.details: Running init.sh from $FRAMEWORK exits 1 with 'Refusing to operate inside the Solo Orchestrator framework repo.' (PR #18 fix holds)

framework_bugs.summary: Platform module filename mismatch: mcp_server vs mcp-server
framework_bugs.actual: init.sh:1124 builds path docs/platform-modules/mcp_server.md, but framework source ships mcp-server.md (hyphen). No platform module is copied. verify-install.sh also reports the file as missing under the underscore name.

documentation_gaps.gap: Runbook expectation 'release_workflow_created' contradicts init.sh behavior for --git-host other (init.sh:2308 deliberately skips release template). Either the runbook should mark this assertion N/A for git_host=other, or init.sh should lay down a host-agnostic placeholder.
documentation_gaps.where: RUNBOOK.md Section A test cases / init.sh:2308

documentation_gaps.gap: process-checklist.sh --start-phase1 is implemented but not listed in --help output (only --start-phase3 and --start-phase4 are listed)

tests.name: each_phase1_step_completes
tests.details: all 5 steps (architecture_selected, threat_model_complete, data_model_defined, ui_scaffolding_done, bible_synthesized) completed RC=0

tests.name: phase_state_advances_to_2
tests.details: U-D confirmation: no script in scripts/ advances .current_phase. Had to manually jq-edit phase-state.json to proceed. check-phase-gate.sh only consistency-checks, never writes.

framework_bugs.summary: U-D confirmation: no script advances current_phase after gate passes
framework_bugs.reproduction: Complete all phase1_architecture steps; bash check-phase-gate.sh reports consistent; jq .current_phase still returns 0. No --advance flag exists on any script.
framework_bugs.expected: Some script (likely check-phase-gate.sh --advance or process-checklist.sh --advance-phase) should write the new phase number into phase-state.json after the gate passes and approval is logged.
framework_bugs.actual: User/agent must edit .claude/phase-state.json directly. This is the same U-D bug from the 2026-04-25 sweep, still open.

tests.name: pre_commit_passes_after_loop
tests.details: git commit succeeded after all 5 steps complete

tests.name: bl006_blocks_feat_without_loop
tests.details: U-C confirmation: direct shell `git commit --allow-empty -m 'feat(bl006-trigger): no loop'` succeeded with no Build Loop active. Calling process-checklist.sh --check-commit-message manually correctly emits the FAIL message, but PreToolUse-only enforcement leaves shell git open.

tests.name: bl006_grace_window_resets_after_record
tests.details: After feature_recorded, build_loop is reset to null. Starting a new --start-feature and walking the 5 steps puts the gate in pass state for a fresh feat: commit (PR #17 between-features grace fix holds).

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_blocks_commit
tests.details: With valid pending-approval.json present, feeding the PreToolUse hook input to pre-commit-gate.sh returns deny with rich reason (question/options/recommendation). Direct shell git commit bypasses the hook (same U-C surface), but the hook itself is correctly wired.

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_resolves
tests.details: scripts/pending-approval.sh --resolve removes the sentinel; subsequent hook invocation no longer denies on sentinel grounds.

framework_bugs.summary: U-C confirmation: BL-006 git commit-msg hook missing for shell commits
framework_bugs.reproduction: git commit --allow-empty -m 'feat(x): no loop' (no Build Loop active, no PreToolUse hook in path)
framework_bugs.expected: commit blocked at git layer (e.g., commit-msg hook) so shell-direct commits cannot bypass the BL-006 gate
framework_bugs.actual: Commit succeeds. Enforcement only happens via Claude Code's PreToolUse hook. Same as U-C from 2026-04-25 sweep, still open.

tests.name: start_phase3_exits_zero
tests.details: process-checklist.sh --start-phase3 RC=0 (with attestation warning expected for phase2-skip)

tests.name: uat_template_lints_clean
tests.details: Even after substituting all documented placeholders (__SESSION_TITLE__, __SESSION_DATE__, __SESSION_FEATURES__, __TESTER_PRE_FLIGHT__, __FEATURE_SECTIONS__, __SCENARIOS_JSON__, __FEATURE_OPTIONS__), one violation remains because line 142 contains a comment '// Do not modify any code outside the __PLACEHOLDER__ tokens.' and the linter flags any string matching __WORD__ regardless of comment context. Linter exits 1 on any unreplaced __X__ token so users can never get a clean exit on the as-shipped template by replacing only the documented placeholders.

tests.name: phase3_to_phase4_gate
tests.details: All 9 PHASE3_STEPS completed once SAST/chaos/a11y/perf/contract result stubs were laid down in docs/test-results/ per the artifact-check rules in process-checklist.sh:310-413

framework_bugs.summary: lint-uat-scenarios.sh false-positive on instructional comment line 142
framework_bugs.reproduction: cp tests/uat/templates/test-session-template.html /tmp/x.html; sed -i '' 's/__SESSION_TITLE__/X/g' (and all other documented placeholders); bash scripts/lint-uat-scenarios.sh /tmp/x.html
framework_bugs.expected: Linter exits 0 once all documented placeholders are replaced
framework_bugs.actual: Linter exits 1 because line 142 'Do not modify any code outside the __PLACEHOLDER__ tokens.' contains the literal token __PLACEHOLDER__ in an instructional comment. Either the linter should ignore tokens inside HTML/JS comments, or the template should rephrase that instruction without the literal token.

tests.name: start_phase4_exits_zero
tests.details: private_poc correctly blocks Phase 4: 'Phase 4 (production release) is blocked — project is in private poc mode.' This is expected/by-design behavior, not a failure. Phase 4 walk is N/A for private_poc.

tests.name: each_phase4_step_completes
tests.details: N/A for private_poc — Phase 4 is unreachable without --to-production upgrade. Recorded as N/A per scenario.

documentation_gaps.gap: Runbook Section E does not call out that private_poc/sponsored_poc agents will hit the by-design Phase 4 block; it reads as if all base agents complete Phase 4.

tests.name: no_uncommitted_secrets
tests.details: grep matches in committed files are all word-references in framework templates / docs (e.g., 'GITHUB_TOKEN' env var names, 'API key configuration' guidance) — no actual high-entropy secret values found

tests.name: log_files_consistent
tests.details: N/A — no FORCE/reset events occurred; .claude/process-audit.log absent (correct)

tests.name: no_framework_contamination
tests.details: git -C $FRAMEWORK status --short shows only ?? .claude/ and ?? Reports/uat-2026-04-26/ (untracked, user-controlled). HEAD unchanged: d66e8f276079aa352ba90ac54386e1b1896d7494.

summary: Base/private_poc/mcp_server/light: init.sh --non-interactive succeeded; phases 0-3 walkable; PR #17/#18/#19 fixes hold (pending-approval helper copied, framework-self guard active, BL-006 grace window resets, BL-015 sentinel via hook works). Three Medium-severity findings: platform-module filename mismatch (mcp_server vs mcp-server), lint-uat-scenarios false positive on instructional comment, U-C and U-D confirmations from prior sweep. Phase 4 correctly blocked for private_poc.
